perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be in effect for one year from the Effective Date and will automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods unless terminated as provided below.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be in effect for one year from the Effective Date and will only renew for successive one (1) year periods if both parties provide written consent to renew, regardless of termination clauses elsewhere.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement auto-renews unless terminated. The changed text makes renewal contingent on explicit written consent from both parties, irrespective of other termination possibilities, thus introducing a contradiction. This contradicts the 'automatic renewal' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. TERM. This Agreement will be in effect for one year from the Effective Date and will only renew for successive one (1) year periods if both parties provide written consent to renew, regardless of termination clauses elsewhere. B. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately if (i) the other party becomes insolvent, files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy, or ceases doing business; or (ii) the other party fails to cure a material breach of the Agreement within thirty (30) days after receipt of written notice of such breach from the party not in default. Upon termination of the Agreement by NETGEAR for Distributor's breach, NETGEAR may cancel all of Distributor's unfulfilled orders without further obligation. This Agreement may be terminated at any time without cause by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that renewal is contingent on written consent from both parties, irrespective of other termination possibilities. This directly contradicts the automatic renewal aspect present in the original text of both the changed text and the contradicted text. The contradiction is further reinforced by the phrase 'regardless of termination clauses elsewhere', which overrules the termination clause present in the 'TERMINATION' part of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Except as may be specifically authorized in writing by NETGEAR, and subject to NETGEAR's continuing approval of Distributor's credit status and financial condition, Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for the initial order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for any order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR, unless NETGEAR decides otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines two payment terms: standard and initial orders. The modified text introduces uncertainty, implying the payment term can vary for 'any' order at NETGEAR's discretion. This contradicts the original term and introduces ambiguity in the payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. PAYMENT. Distributor will pay NETGEAR for all Products ordered by Distributor within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR. The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that payment for any order placed by Distributor under this Agreement shall be due and payable within [*] of the date the corresponding invoice is issued by NETGEAR, unless NETGEAR decides otherwise. NETGEAR, in its sole discretion, reserves the right to specify, and to change from time to time, Distributor's credit line and payment terms. All payments are to be made in U.S. dollars. Payment for shipments made outside of the United States shall be made by wire transfer in accordance with wire transfer procedures provided by NETGEAR. If at any time Distributor is delinquent in the payment of any invoice, exceeds the credit line established by NETGEAR, or is otherwise in breach of the Agreement, NETGEAR may, in its discretion, withhold shipment (including partial shipments) of any order or may require Distributor to pay cash on delivery for further shipments. Payment not received by NETGEAR when due may be subject to a late payment INGRAM NETGEAR Distributor Agreement 3 083096 service charge provided NETGEAR has provided 10 days written notice to Distributor of Distributor's failure to pay. The foregoing notwithstanding, Distributor shall not be deemed in default under this provision if Distributor withholds payment of amounts legitimately in dispute on any invoice provided that (1) Distributor promptly pays the undisputed portion of the invoice in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (2) Distributor provides NETGEAR with written notice of the disputed amount within 10 days of receipt of the invoice; and (3) Distributor works in good faith with NETGEAR to resolve any dispute within a reasonable time period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an element of uncertainty, granting NETGEAR the discretion to alter payment terms for 'any' order, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's attempt to establish clear payment terms. While the original text specified payment terms for initial and standard orders, the modification introduces a clause that undermines the enforceability of previously agreed-upon payment terms, creating a contradiction. NETGEAR can decide the payment terms 'otherwise', creating a potential conflict and uncertainty. This aligns with the structural flaws definition, where the clause is placed in a location with conflicting implications.
